title: eritrichium glomeratum
intro: Eritrichium glomeratum, also known as the clustered alfalfa or clump-headed erythrichium, is a perennial plant in the borage family that grows in mountainous regions with cold climates. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Eritrichium glomeratum has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve for topical use, and its medicinal properties make it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
